etymology.description: 帀 (zā, "to surround") -> inverted 之 (footprint) representing 'surround' sense
etymology.interpretation: The character 帀 (zā, "to surround") is a phono-semantic compound where the component 之, originally written as 㞢, represents a footprint. This indicates the idea of surrounding or encircling with connected steps or pathways, as if forming a boundary or enclosure. This imagery may also allude to the convergence of paths or borders, thus reinforcing the meaning 'to surround.'
